content:
Welcome back folks.
It's a short little review on the stock index futures for today's trading on Monday.
I won't be doing anything on the dollar Index or the Forex market today. I'm not feeling so well, so I'm going
to try to get some rest and hopefully I'll be back in the swing of things tomorrow and we'll do that.

- INITIAL ANALYSIS
The left-hand side chart is the S&P 500 daily chart.
You can see we had a small Sell Side Imbalance Buy Side Inefficiency between those candles low, this
candle's high, and the midpoint of that is Consequent Encroachment. That's what that line is there.

- INTERMEDIATE TERM OUTLOOK
After taking sell side here on the S&P, do we move higher tonight and tomorrow drawing towards this Fair
Value Gap?
I'd like to see that stay open. That would act in a manner that would be bearish, allowing for continuation on
the downside. It's perfectly reasonable for it to come up into this area here, even allowing that little bit of a
wick outside of the inefficiency, it's completely permissible.
So, nothing here has changed in terms of lower prices on the intermediate term for me. That doesn't mean
I won't take a long, and it's not intended to inspire you to do one direction or the other. I'm just airing my
opinion at the moment.

- FVG AND PRICE ACTION
Same way on the NASDAQ on the right-hand side daily chart.
We've taken sell side below here, all this back and forth price action. Okay.
It's reasonable for it to trade down there and then come back up into the inefficiency. It went outside of it
here, it got real close to trading into this one.

- BEARISH SCENARIO AND LIQUIDITY TARGET
If one of these indices are going to do either this Fair Value Gap or this Fair Value Gap, NASDAQ will
probably be the one that does it.
It shouldn't do it. I would like to see it repel price and start moving lower from there. I don't want to see it
trade there and go above, because if it goes above it, it might have to support and then we have to start
considering this area here for buy side as a draw.
But right now, I'm maintaining the opinion that intermediate term I'm hunting something for intermediate
term liquidity down into this inefficiency here.

- DAILY SIBI REFERENCE
For our chart, say the Sell Side Inefficiency, which is a Fair Value Gap, trades up into it here on the 17th.
We sell off.
This red area that's shaded again, that's that daily, it's a Fair Value Gap, specifically a Sell Side Imbalance
Buy Side Inefficiency. Its midpoint is Consequent Encroachment. That's what's being highlighted here with
that line.
So we took sell side on daily chart, consolidated, took a lower low here, then rallied back up into the daily
chart's Sell Side Imbalance Buy Side Inefficiency, turning up into the bearish order block here and then
consolidating into the close.

- DAILY SIBI AND FVG
The four-hour chart and again, the shaded area here for the NASDAQ chart is its respective Sell Side
Imbalance Buy Side Inefficiency from the daily chart. Midpoint is Consequent Encroachment.
Note that we traded down into this small little Fair Value Gap there. Tune into that. So you want to annotate
that on your chart. I'm going to try to keep things as clean as possible so that way you can add them to
your own charts.

I know some of you are probably just simply taking my charts and then writing all over top of them. Now
you're cheating yourself the opportunity to get used to doing it as a daily routine. It's much like a form of
meditation, so it allows you to get closer to the market when you create your own charts and don't look at it
as a time-saving means by just taking my charts. You want to go in and try to set your charts up like mine. It
teaches you how many candlesticks do I keep on my chart? That way it helps you repeat that and get
familiar with doing it.

- NASDAQ VS S&P INEFFICIENCY
But the market comes all the way back down to the low end of daily inefficiency on the NASDAQ, whereas
we went outside of that range on the ES.

- DAILY INEFFICIENCY AND ORDER BLOCK
Hourly chart, you can see we have a Buy Side Reliquification here, daily inefficiency again.
Look how we were respecting it in here. Look at the bodies of that, it's beautiful, and runs down, takes the
sell side here with a lower low, then rallying up to the high of the daily Sell Side Imbalance Buy Side
Inefficiency, specifically aiming into that bearish order block.

- BUY SIDE TARGET AND CRUCIAL PRICE ACTION
On the NASDAQ on the right-hand side, we got real close to taking its perspective buy side, much like we
were just mentioning over here. S&P is a little bit further away. NASDAQ I believe will probably draw up into
that either tonight or tomorrow's morning session, and how we trade there is crucial in my eyes.

- HIGHER LOW AND TIME WINDOWS
Notice that we have the higher low formed on these respective lows here. Can you see that?
So if I'm not mistaken, I believe that's 2 AM to 11 AM, and again 2 AM till 11 AM respectively. Don't take my
word for it. Look at it on your charts, annotate it as such.

- BULLISH BREAKER AND SIBI INTERACTION
We also have a bullish breaker, which is the up-close candle right for the rotation lower. So I have a low,
lower low, we trade higher. We cut through all of this stuff in here because we're not supplying them again.
Cut all the way through, you can see we traded right down into it there beautifully at the time that SMT was
there.
And we wicked outside of that daily Sell Side Imbalance Buy Side Inefficiency, that shaded area pink, and
then rallied all the way back up to aggressively take out the buy side, this buy side, and got real close to
taking this one here.

- BREAKER SENSITIVITY
Same thing over here. Slow, high, lower low, up-close candle. Draw that through all this mess. Gives you an
X-ray view on price. You can see the sensitivity of it right there.

- SMT DIVERGENCE AND NASDAQ TRADE JUSTIFICATION
15-minute time frame again, you can see the SMT Divergence here versus the NASDAQ's higher low.
And I elected to trade the NASDAQ today, and the reason why is because it was respecting its daily
inefficiency. So it went down, respected this, rallied up. The body stayed inside the inefficiency, the wick
went outside of it, and we broke down. And you'll see there's a breaker in here when we get in lower time
frames, and the sell side was obvious as it runs there. Then went to the bullish breaker as just mentioned,
and then after rallies to the upside.

- S&P VS NASDAQ INEFFICIENCY COMPARISON
S&P had a little bit weaker market structure. It wasn't as clear, and it wasn't in as much as a premium as
the NASDAQ did reach. So we had the high of that daily inefficiency reached on that NASDAQ, but we did
not see it here.
All the while, it did go to Consequent Encroachment, which is midpoint of this shaded area, which is
anchored from the daily chart.

- MULTI-TIMEFRAME ANALYSIS AND OPTIMAL SHORT
If you're getting lost in the transitions from one time frame to the next, the wonderful thing about YouTube is
you could just rewind the video or replay it and you'll see how all these levels are simply being transposed
from the daily chart down to the lower time frame.
But we're seeing that respect. Look at the bodies of Consequent Encroachment, which is this midpoint of
the shaded area here in pink.
Now, which one went higher inside of their respective daily, which is that pink box here? Did the S&P go to
the high end of its range? No, but the NASDAQ did.

- SELL SIDE ATTACK AND TRADE ENTRIES
So that's a better short. It went lower, attacked the sell side, and there it is. So I did two trades that you'll
see. They were anchored on the analysis I will show on the five-minute chart.

- S&P VS NASDAQ MARKET STRUCTURE COMPARISON
Right, the five-minute chart here on the S&P, you can see it just really was not as organized in terms of its
market structure. We just weren't able to see the things that I would like to see what you would compare
and contrast over here.
So we went to the high end of the daily inefficiency, the Sell Side Imbalance Buy Side Inefficiency. Look at
the bodies respecting it. That's perfect. And that's what I'm looking for. Those are the signatures that help
me decide which Forex pair, like if it's closely correlated markets like the Pound Dollar versus a Euro Dollar
or Aussie Dollar versus New Zealand Dollar, if I'm looking at them in the same capacity I'm doing here.

- CORRELATED MARKETS AND PREMIUM SELECTION
I'm looking for the ones that are showing the respect of the signatures and price action. I'd like to say this is
the better short because it's higher in terms of its daily inefficiency versus the midpoint here. Both are
going to sell off, likely yes, but I want to be in the one that has the higher premium at the time.
Even though the S&P makes a lower low and it has the lower low for the SMT, I don't care about that. I'm
aiming for the sell side here and it goes right there. And look at the bodies again, which one's showing you
the respect? The inefficiency extreme high.

- SHORT-TERM LOW AND BULLISH BREAKER
NASDAQ taking the short-term low here. Sweeps it twice. On this one here, I took the sell side exit. Went
lower, but look at the bodies stopping dead in its tracks on that low. Wicks through, hits the bullish breaker,
and then rallies.
I was asleep during all this, so I wasn't able to really participate. If I would have been, I would have been all
over it right there.

- SECOND TRADE EXIT STRATEGY
And then once it started to trade lower in here, I used that bearish order block there, and it drops them into
that level right there. That gave me my second exit or short.

Mark turns. If you look at this low here, which is inside this buy side inefficiency, it's an Institutional Order
Flow Entry Drill.
Extend this range, this breaker. We're going to extend it over and you'll see that it's hitting the Mean
Threshold of this down-closed candle from high to low. Midpoint is Mean Threshold, it's not Consequent
Encroachment because it's not a wick or it's not a gap. This is Mean Threshold, but it's acting as an
inversion level. So it's creating a level that would act as where it was over here, turn the sell side of the
curve, it's bearish, but it's a bearish breaker that's become an inversion level where midpoint of that starts a
rally.

- CONSOLIDATION, SILVER BULLET, AND HIGH TARGET
All the consolidation here finally accelerates, takes out the high here, and then as you can see, it just
moves in, gives a nice Silver Bullet here for two o'clock. The stop would be below that candle there.
And then realize that just falls short of the high, which I'm going to go back up one time frame, or actually
two time frames, sorry. This high is what's being highlighted with this line here. So I got real close to that.

- FUTURE PRICE ACTION
And when we moved back down into these inefficiencies, and we'll see if we want to get up there tonight in
London or into tomorrow's morning session.
If we get back below the Consequent Encroachment of the NASDAQ's daily inefficiency here, we will
resume being bearish. Right now, it's consolidation to modestly bullish on an intraday basis.

- BEARISH RESUMPTION AND SWING TRADING MODEL
But I'm waiting for the evidence that we're going to see a longer term short on the daily chart. So those
types of trades are swing trading models. They take a little bit of time to develop, and they can have a
series of up-close candles form before the actual setup forms for a longer term Macro Short.

- BEARISH BREAKER IDENTIFICATION
And finally getting to the one-minute chart here, we have a high, low, higher high. The down-closed candle
here that's shaded, that's a bearish breaker. You can see we trade into that here. We trade outside of that
range and right back up into it, and trading right into this lower portion of this range. So it's the high and the
low, that down-closed candle, extend that through, that's your bearish breaker.

- RELATIVE EQUAL HIGHS AND DISINTEREST
Then we have relative equal highs that get taken out in the afternoon, and it drops down.
I didn't, I didn't see anything in the S&P this morning that I wanted to participate in.

- JUDAS SWING AND SELL SIDE CURVE
Now look at the NASDAQ
- high, low, higher high.
We have an inefficiency here projected on the other side of the curve, meaning we're running up as a
Judas, sells off. So now we're on the sell side of the curve.

- INVERSION FVG AND DAILY SIBI
Over here we have an inefficiency projected to the other side. Now it's going to be an Inversion Fair Value
Gap.
If it wouldn't have traded to the high end of this daily Sell Side Imbalance Buy Side Inefficiency, the
rectangle that's shaded red, we're at the high end of it right here.

- INEFFICIENCY VS. INVERSION FVG
If it hadn't done that yet, okay, and say from the high of the shaded area to the low, if it was like this high
but only went as high as here and then it dropped down into something like this, I would have used that
inefficiency to go long, then reach for the high end of that daily inefficiency like it did here. But because it
went to it, this over here now becomes a potential Inversion Fair Value Gap.

- FIRST TRADE EXECUTION
Right, trades outside of it, back up in. Look at the bodies, real close to Consequent Encroachment, which is
half of this inefficiency. We definitely hit it there, the body's respecting it as well, and it accelerates down to
the sell side and into this inefficiency. So that was the first trade based on the framework that's here.

- SECOND TRADE EXECUTION
And we had an inefficiency there. Beautiful run up in, this is a nice Silver Bullet short again, attacking the
sell side here and the sell side here, the lines here and in the bullish breaker level down here. So it digs into
it, that's where I got my second trade's exit.
CONCLUSION
- TRADING SUMMARY AND NEXT STEPS
And I was away from the charts here, and then it ultimately went higher.
So I only did a little bit of work today for the NASDAQ. I didn't touch the S&P and I was able to add another
2600 onto the Live account. I showed that on Twitter here, welcome to take a look at that.
But I'm going to get some rest and I know I'm under the weather because I'm wanting sleep and I don't
usually want a lot of sleep, and I didn't wanting a lot of sleep all day today. So let me get back to it and I'll
talk to you tomorrow. Be safe.
